platform.heading: Robots, fleet software, and plant intelligence, one closed loop
platform.subtitle: Field robots are data and execution infrastructure, not the endpoint. NECTYR is the live operations layer; ORION extends plant health on the same rails.
platform.loopClosing: Sense → Schedule → Clean → Log → Learn, every cycle across your fleet.
platform.investorTitle: Building India's autonomous operations layer for utility-scale solar
platform.investorBody: 5 GW+ of live fleet capacity, 11 billion+ panel cleaning passes each year feeding fleet AI, recurring AMC relationships across deployed robots, and ORION designed to attach on renewal rails we already operate. TAYPRO's AI learns from every cleaning cycle across the active fleet — a data corpus no competitor can replicate without equivalent field presence at utility scale. See scale, leadership, and how we compound operations data site by site.

feature0.title: Recover lost MWh
feature0.description: Soiling and blind O&M can cost 8–25% of annual generation on Indian utility plants. Waterless robotic cleaning recovers yield with predictable, data-logged cycles, not calendar crews alone.

feature1.title: Consistent performance ratio
feature1.description: Scheduled dry cycles help keep performance ratio stable through dusty seasons, without water logistics or crew availability gaps.

feature2.title: Highest uptime guarantee
feature2.description: Taypro targets industry-leading robot availability with pan-India spares, NECTYR remote diagnostics, and structured AMC support.

feature3.title: Same-day breakdown resolution
feature3.description: Field engineers and regional inventory support same-day on-site response across India, backed by NECTYR ticketing.

feature0.title: Patented dual-pass cleaning
feature0.description: Airflow lifts dry dust first, then microfiber completes the wipe, see our cleaning technology page for the full methodology.

feature1.title: RF mesh & fleet connectivity
feature1.description: LTE, Wi-Fi, hybrid RF mesh, LoRa, and LoRaWAN, sized per site so robots and NECTYR stay connected in the field.

feature2.title: AI/ML scheduling
feature2.description: Automatic cleaning robots adjust cycle cadence from weather and soiling inputs, fewer wasted runs, more predictable O&M.

feature3.title: Made in India
feature3.description: Designed and manufactured at our Chakan, Pune hub with TÜV NORD certified hardware and patents on core cleaning systems.

feature4.title: Self-learning fleet AI
feature4.description: AI that improves cleaning decisions with each cycle across the full fleet — soiling prediction, timing, and routing compound with every site added.

feature5.title: Wet microfiber detection
feature5.description: Sensors detect when cleaning elements are wet from dew or humidity and postpone cycles automatically — protecting module glass from smear damage.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subheading: Quick answers about Taypro cleaning robots in India.
faq.q0: What is a solar panel cleaning robot?
faq.a0: A solar panel cleaning robot autonomously removes dust and soiling from PV modules, typically without water. On Indian utility-scale plants, soiling can suppress generation significantly; robotic dry cleaning helps recover yield with predictable cycles and lower O&M friction than manual crews.
faq.q1: Which solar panel cleaning robot is right for my plant?
faq.a1Before: Fixed and seasonal-tilt utility plants typically use fully automatic waterless robots. Single-axis tracker sites need tracker-compatible autonomous robots. Scattered or smaller blocks often suit semi-automatic portable robots. You can also choose robotic cleaning as a managed Opex service. Compare options on the
faq.a2: No. Taypro uses patented dual-pass dry cleaning, airflow plus microfiber, so you avoid water tankers, module thermal shock, and scarcity constraints in dry regions.
faq.q3: How do I estimate ROI before buying?
faq.a3: Use the free solar panel cleaning robot ROI calculator on taypro.in, then contact our applications team with your layout for a plant-specific quote and SLA draft.
faq.q4: Where are Taypro robots made and supported?
faq.a4: Taypro designs and manufactures in Chakan, Pune, with pan-India commissioning, spares, and same-day breakdown targets backed by NECTYR remote diagnostics.
faq.q5: How do solar panel cleaning robots compare to manual O&M?
faq.a5: Robots deliver predictable dry-cleaning cycles without water tankers, crew scheduling gaps, or safety risk on large arrays, while logging every pass in NECTYR. Manual teams can work for small blocks; utility-scale portfolios typically move to autonomous robots or Taypro Opex for TCO and uptime. Compare models on the solar panel cleaning robots hub.
faq.q6: Should we buy robots (CAPEX) or use Taypro Opex?
faq.a6: Both paths are available. CAPEX gives you owned hardware with Taypro commissioning and AMC. Taypro Opex is pay-per-panel cleaning with Taypro operating the fleet, useful when you want predictable O&M without upfront robot capital. The commercial model section on our solar panel cleaning system hub walks through all five engagement options.
